hints: Complemento: Para verter en el café (finalidad/propósito)
hintDisplay: Sujeto: Hay (construcción impersonal) / Verbo: Haber (existencia, en presente negativo) / Complemento: Leche (sustantivo, objeto directo) / Complemento: Para verter en el café (finalidad/propósito)
spanishSentence: No hay leche para verter en el café.
correctSentence: There is no milk to pour into the coffee.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: Varios aperitivos / Verbo: Haber (existencia) / Complemento: En el menú / Verbo: Dar / Complemento: Propina al camarero / Verbo: Recomendar / Complemento: Los mejores aperitivos
spanishSentence: Hay varios aperitivos en el menú, así que le daré propina al camarero por recomendar los mejores.
correctSentence: There are several appetizers on the menu, so I will tip the waiter for recommending the best ones.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: mucho glaseado / Verbo: haber (existencia) / Complemento: el panadero / Verbo: necesitar / Complemento: aplicar al pastel
spanishSentence: Hay mucho glaseado que el panadero necesita aplicar al pastel.
correctSentence: There is a lot of frosting that the baker needs to apply to the cake.

correctSentence: There are many apples that we eat for breakfast.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: Hay (existencia) / Complemento: muchas verduras / Complemento: que el chef cocina / Complemento: en la cocina / Verbo: haber (existencia) / Verbo: cocinar (acción principal del chef)

correctSentence: There are many vegetables that the chef cooks in the kitchen.

hintDisplay: Sujeto: Muchas manzanas / Verbo: Comer / Complemento: Que como (cláusula relativa) / Tiempo: Todos los días

correctSentence: There are many apples that I eat every day.

correctSentence: There are several potatoes that mom needs to cook for dinner.

correctSentence: There are many delicious pizzas that the guests eat at the party.

correctSentence: There is too much salt in the food that the chef will taste.

correctSentence: There is fresh bread that the waiter will bring to your table.

correctSentence: There is no salt in the food that the guests taste.

correctSentence: There is too much oil in the pan when mom tries to fry the chicken.

correctSentence: There are some burgers on the grill that we are cooking for dinner.
